name: Coconut Biscuit Shortcakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
2. In a large bowl, mix the Bisquick baking mix, 3 tablespoons sugar, margarine or butter, cinnamon, and coconut until a soft dough forms.
3. Turn the mixture out onto a surface lightly dusted with additional Bisquick baking mix. Gently roll the dough to coat it in the mix.
4. Shape the dough into a ball and knead it 8 to 10 times.
5. Roll the dough into an even layer that is 1/2 inch thick. Sprinkle with 1 tablespoon sugar.
6. Using a cutter measuring 3 inches, cut the dough into pieces and place them on an ungreased cookie sheet.
7. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until golden brown.
8. In a chilled medium bowl, beat whipping cream and 2 tablespoons sugar until stiff peaks form.
9. Once the shortcakes are warm, split them in half and fill with fresh berries.
10. Top with whipped cream.
Makes 6 shortcakes.
To toast coconut, preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Place the flaked coconut in an ungreased pan and bake for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly golden brown.
